title: Movie Theaters Are Closed. When They Reopen, Will the Movies Follow?
format: Recorded in the first weekend of COVID lockdown, as AMC and Alamo Drafthouse go dark and Universal drops three movies to $19.99 VOD.

films.title: Vampires
films.note: John Carpenter's; the last time a weekend was this low was 1998, when it led with $55M.

films.title: Lost Girls
films.note: No. 2 on Netflix's top 10 and, per Sean, watched by no one he knows.

films.title: Never Rarely Sometimes Always
films.note: Eliza Hittman's Sundance film opened to $18,000; the small-movie casualty they both mourn.

films.title: Onward
films.note: No. 1 at $10M with the biggest second-week drop in Pixar history.

films.title: I Still Believe
films.note: The Christian melodrama that still pulled $9.5M — the only thing stronger than coronavirus.

films.title: Bloodshot
films.note: Vin Diesel comic-book movie at $9.3M, one of the century's worst comic-book openings.

films.title: The Invisible Man
films.note: Already a hit and near the end of its run; the savviest of Universal's three VOD moves.

films.title: The Hunt
films.note: The cursed satire; releasing it online is smart precisely because it's steeped in discourse.

films.title: Emma
films.note: Was supposed to widen to a thousand theaters this weekend; instead goes to VOD.

films.title: Frozen II
films.note: Added to Disney+ three months early — a salve for parents locked in at home.

films.title: Mulan
films.note: A senior Disney exec told the NYT a Disney+ pivot wasn't under discussion, citing piracy; days later, delayed.

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: Pushed to iTunes early; Sean won't be rewatching.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: The Broccoli family got ahead of the curve with a November date; too big to ever price at $19.99.

films.title: Trolls World Tour
films.note: Day-and-date theaters + all platforms on April 10; floated as the first big-picture live Zoom watch.

films.title: F9
films.note: Universal moved Fast 9 to next spring for room to restart its marketing.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: The one everyone asks about; six weeks out and the most consequential release left on the calendar.

segments.heading: One Movie We'd Take Straight to VOD
segments.note: one host, one pick — big tentpoles excepted

picks.title: Death on the Nile
picks.note: Kenneth Branagh's second Agatha Christie; she doesn't care about the timeline, just release it.

picks.title: The Many Saints of Newark
picks.note: The Sopranos prequel — functionally a TV show, so he's happy to watch it at home.

picks.title: Under the Tuscan Sun
picks.note: Her group-chat live-watch pick; strange enough to text about, not good enough to require silence.

picks.title: The American President
picks.note: Reiner-directed, Sorkin-written West Wing dry run and an underrated '90s rom-com.

picks.title: Magic
picks.note: William Goldman's ventriloquist horror with Anthony Hopkins; an extended Twilight Zone episode.

picks.title: Fail Safe
picks.note: The most intense movie ever made — every shot under some man's nose in a war room.

picks.title: All the President's Men
picks.note: People in a room solving problems and yelling; Goldman comfort food.

films: El Camino: A Breaking Bad Movie

TV: The Falcon and the Winter Soldier

Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
